title: Remote Desktop and Screen-Sharing Setup
text:
Q: How do I set up remote desktop and screen-sharing on Windows and Mac?
A: To set up remote desktop on Windows, go to 'Settings', then 'System', and select 'Remote Desktop'. Turn on the 'Enable Remote Desktop' option and note the PC name. For Mac, open 'System Preferences', click on 'Sharing', and check 'Screen Sharing' to enable it. You can access the remote desktop from another device using the Remote Desktop Connection app on Windows or the Screen Sharing app on Mac. Make sure both devices are connected to the internet and that you have the necessary permissions. For screen-sharing, you may also consider using applications like Zoom or Microsoft Teams, which offer built-in screen-sharing features.
